user_query: WAN crashes after 121 frames but images work fine. What should I check?

cards.title: Video jobs scale differently from still images
cards.canonical_summary: Video pipelines often fail at frame counts or batch sizes that still-image workflows handle easily because temporal workloads amplify VRAM pressure.
cards.source_priority_rule: Video and VRAM guidance outrank image-only instincts.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that temporal workloads change memory behavior.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat video like a still-image job.

cards.title: Out of memory during longer runs
cards.canonical_summary: OOM during longer video runs strongly suggests frame count, batch size, or cache behavior needs adjustment.
cards.source_priority_rule: Runtime logs and lane guidance outrank intuition.
cards.answer_pattern: Route to memory tuning and test smaller frame counts first.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not send OOM errors to unrelated lanes.

cards.title: VRAM issue vs runtime install issue
cards.canonical_summary: If the error is a direct OOM or scaling failure, route here. If the model never starts because of import/runtime errors, route to runtime alignment.
cards.source_priority_rule: Exact error text drives the branch.
cards.answer_pattern: Differentiate memory pressure from runtime setup failure.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not merge install failures into memory tuning.

cards.title: Trust runtime evidence for memory claims
cards.canonical_summary: For memory issues, runtime logs and measured behavior outrank memory-based advice and old tuning notes.
cards.source_priority_rule: Measured evidence outranks general advice.
cards.answer_pattern: State that observed runtime behavior should lead the diagnosis.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not overrule live evidence with stale lore.

cards.title: Last-frame chaining extends video in chunks
cards.canonical_summary: Use the final frame of one segment as the start image for the next segment.
cards.source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain chunking and last-frame carryover.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact commands, paths, class names, model names, or source claims without retrieved evidence.

context_pack.routing_rule: Primary lane first. Secondary lane only if a selected card explicitly supports cross-lane reference.
context_pack.notes: Generated seed sample for training-plan validation.

user_query: Why will these ComfyUI nodes not connect? It says input type mismatch.

cards.title: IMAGE and LATENT are different types
cards.canonical_summary: In ComfyUI, IMAGE and LATENT are distinct data types. Many node connection failures come from crossing that boundary incorrectly.
cards.source_priority_rule: Installed object_info or source-backed node definitions outrank memory for exact type or class claims.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain the datatype boundary and recommend checking output/input socket types.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact conversion node names without evidence.

cards.title: Node connection says input type mismatch
cards.canonical_summary: A direct input-type mismatch message strongly suggests a socket contract problem rather than a missing package or model.
cards.source_priority_rule: Failure signatures outrank broad troubleshooting habits.
cards.answer_pattern: Route first to socket-contract debugging and only then inspect neighboring causes.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not send the user to dependency troubleshooting first.

cards.title: Socket mismatch vs Python dependency issue
cards.canonical_summary: If the message mentions import failures or missing modules, route to runtime/dependency lanes. If it mentions input/output mismatch, route here.
cards.source_priority_rule: Error-message text should drive routing first.
cards.answer_pattern: Differentiate the failure class and route accordingly.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not mix environment setup failures with socket contract failures.

cards.title: Exact socket claims need source-backed definitions
cards.canonical_summary: For exact socket, class_type, or connection claims, installed object_info and source-backed node definitions outrank memory and archive notes.
cards.source_priority_rule: Installed definitions outrank memory and older notes.
cards.answer_pattern: State the source rule before offering precision advice.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact node names or socket layouts.

cards.title: Workflow repair starts with graph validity
cards.canonical_summary: Repair should first check graph validity before changing model settings.
cards.source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Start with graph validity and missing-node checks.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact commands, paths, class names, model names, or source claims without retrieved evidence.

context_pack.routing_rule: Primary lane first. Secondary lane only if a selected card explicitly supports cross-lane reference.
context_pack.notes: Generated seed sample for training-plan validation.

user_query: Torch says CUDA is unavailable but I have an NVIDIA GPU. Is this an OOM issue?

cards.title: Torch, CUDA, and driver must align
cards.canonical_summary: GPU acceleration depends on compatible driver, CUDA runtime, PyTorch build, and optional acceleration libraries.
cards.source_priority_rule: Current official install guidance and local diagnostic output outrank old setup notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain alignment as a stack, not a single setting.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim a specific install command is current without verification.

cards.title: PyTorch reports CUDA unavailable
cards.canonical_summary: This failure means the active Python environment cannot currently use CUDA through PyTorch.
cards.source_priority_rule: Live diagnostic output drives routing.
cards.answer_pattern: Recommend checking active interpreter, torch build, and driver visibility.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not immediately reinstall everything.

cards.title: CUDA unavailable vs VRAM OOM
cards.canonical_summary: CUDA unavailable means the environment cannot access GPU support; OOM means it can access the GPU but the job exceeds available memory.
cards.source_priority_rule: Exact error wording determines route.
cards.answer_pattern: Separate access failure from memory capacity failure.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not solve CUDA unavailable by lowering batch size.

cards.title: Official/current install docs win for version commands
cards.canonical_summary: For exact install commands and current package versions, official/current PyTorch or package documentation outranks archived notes.
cards.source_priority_rule: Current official docs and live diagnostics outrank memory.
cards.answer_pattern: State that exact commands must be verified before use.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not freeze stale install commands into the answer.

cards.title: Windows paths are part of the AI runtime
cards.canonical_summary: Windows AI setups often fail because paths, drive letters, permissions, and launch directories do not match what the tool expects.
cards.source_priority_rule: Local filesystem evidence and current config files outrank memory-based path assumptions.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that path layout is a runtime dependency and recommend verifying the actual configured path.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent local drive paths or assume a user's folder layout.

cards.title: Central model storage prevents duplicate downloads
cards.canonical_summary: A central model repository reduces duplicate downloads and keeps multiple AI apps pointed at the same assets.

cards.exactness_guard: Do not assume every tool uses the same folder schema.

cards.title: Checkpoint not found
cards.canonical_summary: A checkpoint-not-found error usually means the app cannot see the model file at the expected path.
cards.source_priority_rule: Actual file path and app config drive diagnosis.
cards.answer_pattern: Check whether the file exists and whether the app path points to it.

cards.source_priority_rule: File existence and configured paths drive branch.
cards.answer_pattern: Separate absent assets from invisible assets.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not re-download files that are already present and unmapped.

cards.title: Filesystem and config are source of truth
cards.canonical_summary: For local model-folder issues, the actual filesystem, app config, and runtime logs outrank remembered default paths.
cards.source_priority_rule: Observed local configuration wins.
cards.answer_pattern: State source order and verify before moving files.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent drive letters or delete paths without confirmation.

cards.title: Windows paths are part of the AI runtime
cards.canonical_summary: Windows AI setups often fail because paths, drive letters, permissions, and launch directories do not match what the tool expects.
cards.source_priority_rule: Local filesystem evidence and current config files outrank memory-based path assumptions.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that path layout is a runtime dependency and recommend verifying the actual configured path.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent local drive paths or assume a user's folder layout.

user_query: How do we prove Atlas RAG is better than raw RAG without just guessing?

cards.title: Raw RAG is a baseline, not the enemy
cards.canonical_summary: Raw RAG provides a baseline for comparison. The project should prove whether Atlas structure improves results rather than assume it.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evaluation docs outrank preference for the Atlas idea.
cards.answer_pattern: Frame raw RAG as the control condition.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim Atlas is better without results.

cards.title: Atlas answer cites weak or wrong card
cards.canonical_summary: If Atlas RAG retrieves or follows a weak card, the lane/card system needs refinement rather than blame being placed only on the model.
cards.source_priority_rule: Failure analysis drives the fix.
cards.answer_pattern: Identify whether retrieval, card quality, or model behavior caused the issue.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not hide bad retrieval cases.

cards.title: Retrieval problem vs generation problem
cards.canonical_summary: If the right evidence was not retrieved, fix retrieval/lane/card design. If evidence was retrieved but ignored, inspect model behavior or adapter training.
cards.source_priority_rule: Logs and retrieved context drive branch.
cards.answer_pattern: Separate retrieval failure from generation failure.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not change training data before checking retrieved context.

cards.title: Evaluation results outrank architecture theory
cards.canonical_summary: Measured evaluation results and saved raw outputs outrank the project's expected benefit.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evidence from runs outranks concept confidence.
cards.answer_pattern: State that test data decides the claim.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim success before scored results exist.

cards.title: Benchmark small models against larger baselines
cards.canonical_summary: Small-model claims should be tested against larger baselines using the same prompts and retrieval conditions.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evaluation results outrank expectations about model size.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain paired benchmarking and why same-question comparisons matter.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim 4B equals 7B without results.

context_pack.routing_rule: Primary lane first. Secondary lane only if a selected card explicitly supports cross-lane reference.

cards.title: Some claims expire quickly
cards.canonical_summary: Tool versions, APIs, model availability, pricing, licenses, and security guidance can change quickly.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain volatility and identify whether verification is needed.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat fast-moving claims as stable.

cards.title: Current tool claim from old notes
cards.canonical_summary: A current claim about an active tool based on old notes should be flagged for verification.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Mark as volatile and verify before recommending.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not present old tool behavior as current.

cards.title: Stable concept vs volatile implementation
cards.canonical_summary: Principles may be stable; versions, APIs, availability, and licenses require verification.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Branch by claim category.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not verify or ignore freshness blindly.

cards.title: Freshness source priority
cards.canonical_summary: For volatile claims, current official docs, repos, model cards, release notes, and local outputs outrank archives or memory.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: State source rule before current guidance.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not cite stale sources as final authority.

cards.title: Source ledger tracks provenance
cards.canonical_summary: The source ledger records where claims come from so Atlas can distinguish evidence from memory or drafts.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain provenance as the backbone of source-governed RAG.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat unsourced cards as verified.

cards.title: What a retrieval card is
cards.canonical_summary: A retrieval card is a compact, source-aware signpost inside a lane. It helps the model route, interpret, and answer a specific problem pattern.
cards.source_priority_rule: Card schema and lane overview outrank ad-hoc examples.
cards.answer_pattern: Define the card, explain its role, and connect it to routing and answer behavior.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent schema fields or card roles not present in the project.

cards.title: Cards are signposts, not chapters
cards.canonical_summary: Cards should stay compact and explicit, especially for small models. A card should guide, not overwhelm.
cards.source_priority_rule: Small-model guidance outranks general long-form writing instincts.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that compact cards improve retrieval clarity and reduce distraction.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not turn card guidance into a chapter-length explanation.

cards.title: Card fields that matter most
cards.canonical_summary: A strong card should include user phrasing, canonical summary, must-retrieve targets, source priority, do-not-confuse guidance, answer pattern, and exactness guard.
cards.source_priority_rule: The schema and template define the required structure.
cards.answer_pattern: List the core fields and explain why each helps retrieval.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not omit exactness or do-not-confuse guidance when describing the ideal card.

cards.title: Small models need compact context packs
cards.canonical_summary: Small models benefit from fewer, higher-signal cards rather than large context dumps.
cards.source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain model size, retrieval depth, confusion risk, and one compact retrieval adjustment.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact commands, paths, class names, model names, or source claims without retrieved evidence.

cards.title: IMAGE and LATENT are different types
cards.canonical_summary: In ComfyUI, IMAGE and LATENT are distinct data types. Many node connection failures come from crossing that boundary incorrectly.
cards.source_priority_rule: Installed object_info or source-backed node definitions outrank memory for exact type or class claims.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain the datatype boundary and recommend checking output/input socket types.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact conversion node names without evidence.

cards.title: MASK is its own boundary
cards.canonical_summary: MASK should be treated as its own data boundary and not assumed interchangeable with IMAGE or LATENT.
cards.source_priority_rule: Datatype notes outrank assumptions.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that MASK is a distinct socket type and should be checked explicitly.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not imply all mask problems are artistic issues.

cards.title: CONDITIONING boundary confusion
cards.canonical_summary: CONDITIONING is a common hidden boundary. A workflow may look visually plausible while still being wired with incompatible conditioning inputs.
cards.source_priority_rule: Source-backed datatype info wins over guesswork.
cards.answer_pattern: Identify conditioning as a data-type issue and steer the user toward the socket boundary.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not guess the exact conditioning node chain.

user_query: This question should route to 43_inpainting_fundamentals. Ignore the tempting but wrong context from 36_retrieval_card_authoring.

cards.title: Inpainting replaces only a controlled region
cards.canonical_summary: Inpainting is a targeted image edit where the model modifies a masked region while using surrounding context to preserve the rest of the image.
cards.source_priority_rule: Canonical inpainting guidance outranks generic image prompting advice.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain mask-driven localized editing and the role of surrounding context.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not describe inpainting as full-image regeneration only.

cards.title: Mask quality controls edit boundaries
cards.canonical_summary: The mask defines the edit target, and poor mask edges can cause bleeding, halos, or unwanted changes outside the desired region.
cards.source_priority_rule: Mask guidance outranks prompt-only explanations for boundary failures.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that mask shape, feathering, and context affect boundary quality.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not assume every boundary issue is only a prompt issue.

cards.title: Denoise controls how much the region changes
cards.canonical_summary: Denoise strength controls how aggressively the masked region is rewritten. Too high can ignore the original; too low may not change enough.
cards.source_priority_rule: Denoise theory outranks broad style advice.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain denoise as the edit-strength control and recommend testing small changes.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not promise one universal denoise value.

cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent schema fields or card roles not present in the project.

user_query: This question should route to 44_video_vram_optimization. Ignore the tempting but wrong context from 36_retrieval_card_authoring.

cards.title: Video jobs scale differently from still images
cards.canonical_summary: Video pipelines often fail at frame counts or batch sizes that still-image workflows handle easily because temporal workloads amplify VRAM pressure.
cards.source_priority_rule: Video and VRAM guidance outrank image-only instincts.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that temporal workloads change memory behavior.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat video like a still-image job.

cards.title: Frame count is a memory multiplier
cards.canonical_summary: Longer frame counts often multiply memory pressure and runtime exposure, especially in video models.
cards.source_priority_rule: Current lane guidance outranks general assumptions.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain the frame-count effect and recommend testing shorter runs.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not imply frame count is a cosmetic choice only.

cards.title: Batching and caching matter
cards.canonical_summary: Frames per batch, caching behavior, and offload settings can determine whether a video run fits in memory.
cards.source_priority_rule: Runtime and workflow docs outrank guesswork.
cards.answer_pattern: Point to batching/cache knobs as first-line memory controls.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not overfocus on prompt wording for OOM-class failures.

cards.title: Last-frame chaining extends video in chunks
cards.canonical_summary: Use the final frame of one segment as the start image for the next segment.
cards.source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain chunking and last-frame carryover.

cards.title: The last frame becomes the next start image

cards.title: Chunk length balances continuity and memory
cards.canonical_summary: Shorter chunks are safer; longer chunks may reduce stitch points but increase VRAM pressure.
cards.source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain memory/continuity tradeoff and test smaller chunks first.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not invent exact commands, paths, class names, model names, or source claims without retrieved evidence.

user_query: This question should route to 46_gradio_multi_venv_orchestration. Ignore the tempting but wrong context from 36_retrieval_card_authoring.

cards.title: One UI can orchestrate multiple isolated tools
cards.canonical_summary: A Gradio front end can act as a control surface while separate backend scripts run in their own virtual environments.
cards.source_priority_rule: Architecture notes and actual process-launch design outrank generic UI examples.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain front-end orchestration vs backend dependency isolation.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not imply one Python process can safely import conflicting stacks.

cards.title: Subprocess boundaries protect dependency stacks
cards.canonical_summary: Launching tools as subprocesses from their own venvs can prevent dependency conflicts from crashing the main UI environment.
cards.source_priority_rule: Process boundary design outranks convenience imports.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain subprocess isolation and why imports are riskier.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not recommend importing every module into the Gradio app.

cards.title: The UI should pass files and parameters, not hidden state
cards.canonical_summary: Multi-venv tools should communicate through explicit files, parameters, JSON, stdout/stderr, or defined APIs rather than hidden shared state.
cards.source_priority_rule: Explicit interface design outranks ad-hoc coupling.
cards.answer_pattern: Recommend explicit interfaces and log capture.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not create invisible coupling that cannot be debugged.

cards.title: Raw RAG is a baseline, not the enemy
cards.canonical_summary: Raw RAG provides a baseline for comparison. The project should prove whether Atlas structure improves results rather than assume it.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evaluation docs outrank preference for the Atlas idea.
cards.answer_pattern: Frame raw RAG as the control condition.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim Atlas is better without results.

cards.title: Atlas RAG adds structure around retrieval
cards.canonical_summary: Atlas RAG adds lanes, cards, source priority, do-not-confuse rules, answer patterns, and evaluation to ordinary retrieval.
cards.source_priority_rule: Project definitions outrank generic RAG descriptions.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain the structural difference clearly.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not say Atlas replaces RAG.

cards.title: The comparison must use same questions
cards.canonical_summary: Raw RAG and Atlas RAG should be tested on the same question set to produce fair comparisons.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evaluation design controls comparison.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain paired testing and same-prompt conditions.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not compare unrelated examples.

cards.title: Benchmark small models against larger baselines
cards.canonical_summary: Small-model claims should be tested against larger baselines using the same prompts and retrieval conditions.
cards.source_priority_rule: Evaluation results outrank expectations about model size.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain paired benchmarking and why same-question comparisons matter.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not claim 4B equals 7B without results.

cards.title: Compare retrieval conditions separately
cards.canonical_summary: Testing should separate no RAG, raw RAG, Atlas RAG, and Atlas Reader LoRA + Atlas RAG conditions.
cards.source_priority_rule: The baseline matrix defines valid comparison conditions.
cards.answer_pattern: List the comparison conditions and keep them separate.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not mix conditions in a single result claim.

cards.title: Small models need bounded tasks
cards.canonical_summary: Small models should be tested on bounded lane/card tasks before broad synthesis tasks.
cards.source_priority_rule: Small-model contract guidance defines the target use case.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain that success is expected in bounded structured-RAG tasks first.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not imply small models can solve all tasks.

cards.title: Negative results are valid research data
cards.canonical_summary: A failed or mixed benchmark still creates useful data about structured RAG, small-model routing, and adapter limits.
cards.source_priority_rule: Research-value policy outranks success-only framing.
cards.answer_pattern: Frame failure as data and identify what it teaches.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not hide or relabel failed results as success.

context_pack.routing_rule: For volatile claims, qualify or verify. Do not invent current evidence.

cards.title: Source ledger tracks provenance
cards.canonical_summary: The source ledger records where claims come from so Atlas can distinguish evidence from memory or drafts.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain provenance as the backbone of source-governed RAG.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat unsourced cards as verified.

cards.title: Evidence grade matters
cards.canonical_summary: Sources need priority so canonical/current evidence outranks weaker notes or archives.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain trust levels and why chunks are not equal.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not flatten source authority.

cards.title: Source coverage supports lanes

cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain how coverage protects against hallucination.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not call a lane validated without source coverage.

cards.title: Archives are lower-priority memory

cards.answer_pattern: Explain archived-source role and limits.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not delete old evidence just because it is lower priority.

cards.title: Some claims expire quickly
cards.canonical_summary: Tool versions, APIs, model availability, pricing, licenses, and security guidance can change quickly.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain volatility and identify whether verification is needed.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not treat fast-moving claims as stable.

cards.title: Stable principles need less checking
cards.canonical_summary: General principles and internal project definitions can be stable even when exact tool behavior changes.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Separate durable principle from implementation detail.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not over-verify stable project definitions.

cards.title: Freshness changes confidence
cards.canonical_summary: A claim from an old source may be lower confidence if the topic is volatile.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Explain how age and volatility modify confidence.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not call old volatile claims high confidence.

cards.title: Verify the exact claim
cards.canonical_summary: Freshness checks should verify the exact unstable claim, not browse unrelated background material.
cards.source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
cards.answer_pattern: Identify the precise claim and verify that claim.
cards.exactness_guard: Do not replace targeted verification with vague research.
